dataset: Synthetic NTFS disk images created by setup_task.sh

pi_items.source: Verified in task.json description and setup_task.sh cleanup code (glob pattern 'IP_Theft_2024*'). Export_result.sh searches for this pattern. Confirmed consistent across all files.
Verified in task.json description. Agent must type this during case creation. Export script does not verify case number directly.

Live test confirmed: file exists, 16777216 bytes (16MB), owned by ga:ga, permissions 644. Created by setup_task.sh via dd+mkfs.ntfs. Contains 30 files across 5 departments (Engineering/6, Finance/6, Legal/6, Research/6, Executive/6).

Live test confirmed: file exists, 16777216 bytes (16MB), owned by ga:ga, permissions 644. Contains 11 allocated files (8 exact copies + 3 modified), 5 deleted files, 3 NTFS ADS. Verified via fls -r -p output showing all entries.

Live test confirmed: file exists, 1696 bytes, 30 lines, owned by ga:ga, permissions 644. Each line is 'md5_hash  filename' format. All 30 lines validated as having 32-char hex MD5 hashes. Hashes computed at runtime from actual corporate_repo.dd contents using fls+icat+hashlib.md5.
Verified that /home/ga/Reports/ directory exists with ga:ga ownership and 755 permissions. Write test confirmed ga user can create files there. This is where the agent must write the report.

Verified by running export_result.sh in live environment. Script produces valid JSON at this path with all expected fields (case_db_found, data_source_count, hashset_imported, report_exists, etc.). Output was valid JSON with start_time=1774122537.

Verified in live environment: file exists after setup, chmod 600 owned by root, NOT readable by ga user (anti-gaming confirmed). Contains full ground truth with corporate_files, exfiltrated, modified, deleted, ads, and summary sections.

privileged_info_summary:
The task creates two 16MB NTFS disk images. corporate_repo.dd contains exactly 30 files across 5 departments (Engineering, Finance, Legal, Research, Executive — 6 files each). suspect_usb.dd contains 11 allocated files, 5 deleted files, and 3 NTFS Alternate Data Streams.

EXFILTRATED FILES (8 exact MD5 matches between corporate and USB): (1) circuit_design_v3.txt (MD5: b9663487aa0463e1dbf639cbb5ac2353, 653 bytes, from Engineering), (2) firmware_specs.txt (MD5: ad6012ae573fcf595f75dd19da73404b, 600 bytes, from Engineering), (3) revenue_forecast_2024.txt (MD5: 60da274b46da6ac3f2b8992bc8e4c5b7, 474 bytes, from Finance), (4) patent_draft_2024_001.txt (MD5: 7188b420d72eeedf264f6d3bc3b75121, 784 bytes, from Legal), (5) compound_analysis.csv (MD5: bf51eb475626ca77f2a1b9da8c1ea70e, 576 bytes, from Research), (6) board_strategy_2025.txt (MD5: 2556b67dfad045fbf5bf1b2e2c92cecd, 759 bytes, from Executive), (7) acquisition_target_list.txt (MD5: ce2855136dce96b9c3fa1ac331b2379b, 785 bytes, from Executive), (8) executive_compensation.csv (MD5: fcbb6d71c8f6fbe9641e7544ad5ca9b5, 413 bytes, from Executive).

MODIFIED COPIES (3 files with same name but different MD5 — USB versions have appended content): (1) sensor_calibration.csv (Corp MD5: c0aab859ca831e27a75c81b6aa8b68ba, USB MD5: bbbe7ca5ddf16275b4a024d1e7ab35be), (2) trial_results_batch_7.txt (Corp MD5: 43468e52d2a8e761c5cb73562d1fe9be, USB MD5: 7e9bdfc5cec9568d26e33b6cc8cae402), (3) market_analysis.txt (Corp MD5: 78ab13b6f553fc9400f668cc1cd55626, USB MD5: 4ca10909023daf1bfcad42f9ba7d930c).

DELETED USB FILES (5 files marked with * in fls output): (1) vendor_payments_q3.csv (inode 77), (2) experiment_log_042.txt (inode 78), (3) personal_notes.txt (inode 79), (4) job_applications.txt (inode 80), (5) usb_transfer_log.txt (inode 81).

CONCEALED DATA — 3 NTFS Alternate Data Streams on USB: (1) circuit_design_v3.txt:transfer_log (107 bytes, content: 'Copied via USB3.0 hub at 22:14 UTC 2024-03-10'), (2) board_strategy_2025.txt:notes (115 bytes, content: 'Review before CompetitorCorp interview on March 20'), (3) compound_analysis.csv:source_path (92 bytes, content: 'Original location: //CORP-NAS/Research/Active/Project_Nimbus/').

INVESTIGATION SUMMARY: 8 exfiltrated, 3 modified, 5 deleted, 3 concealed. Most-targeted department is Executive (3 exfiltrated files: board_strategy_2025.txt, acquisition_target_list.txt, executive_compensation.csv). Risk classification should be HIGH or CRITICAL given the volume and sensitivity of exfiltrated data (board strategy, M&A targets, executive compensation, patent drafts).

The corporate_hashes.txt file contains 30 MD5 hashes (one per corporate file) and should be imported as a hash set named 'Corporate_IP' in Autopsy. The USB files are organized under Documents/ (8 allocated) and Projects/ (3 allocated: compound_analysis.csv, sensor_calibration.csv, trial_results_batch_7.txt). All data is deterministic — file content is hardcoded in setup_task.sh heredocs, so MD5 hashes are consistent across runs.
